stories.summary: The Nifty IT index has rebounded nearly 13.5% from its July 1 low and gained around 5% this week, signalling improving momentum. The 29,600–29,650 zone is the key resistance, while 28,500–28,550 is expected to act as immediate support.

stories.title: Will Sensex, Nifty extend gains on Monday? Q1 earnings, global tech selloff among 6 factors to steer D-St this week
stories.summary: Dalal Street ended the week on a strong note, with the Sensex and Nifty gaining over 1% on Friday. Investors will track six key market triggers this week, including the peak of the Q1 earnings season, the escalating Iran-US conflict, surging crude oil prices, the global tech selloff, rupee movement and FII flows. Analysts remain cautiously optimistic on market sentiment.

stories.title: South Korea to expand use of won-denominated bonds for foreign investors
stories.summary: South Korea has announced plans to enhance global acceptance of its currency by allowing foreign financial institutions to borrow won. Additionally, these institutions will be permitted to utilize won-denominated bonds as collateral. Furthermore, the dollar-won market trading hours have been expanded to twenty-four hours, seeking to integrate South Korea’s currency into the wider international markets.
